lede: A look at March 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Wander Franco and Kesha, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2014 Kunming attack
events.desc: Thirty-five people are killed and 143 injured in a mass stabbing at Kunming Railway Station in China.

events.title: Black September Organization
events.desc: Black September storms the Saudi embassy in Khartoum, Sudan, resulting in the assassination of three Western hostages.

events.title: International Monetary Fund
events.desc: The International Monetary Fund begins financial operations.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Bulgaria signs the Tripartite Pact, allying itself with the Axis powers.

events.title: Australia national cricket team
events.desc: The Australian cricket team captained by Warwick Armstrong becomes the first team to complete a whitewash of The Ashes, something that would not be repeated for 86 years.

events.title: Battle of Adwa
events.desc: Battle of Adwa: An Ethiopian army defeats an outnumbered Italian force, ending the First Italo-Ethiopian War.

events.title: Yellowstone National Park
events.desc: Yellowstone National Park is established as the world's first national park.

events.title: John Tyler
events.desc: United States President John Tyler signs a bill authorizing the United States to annex the Republic of Texas.

events.title: Samuel Chase
events.desc: Justice Samuel Chase is acquitted at the end of his impeachment trial before the U.S. Senate.

births.name: Timothy Laurence
births.line: member of the British royal family and Royal Navy officer

deaths.text: Pat Ingoldsby, Irish poet and television presenter

observances.text: Beer Day, marked the end of beer prohibition in 1989 (Iceland)
Christian feast day: Agnes Tsao Kou Ying (one of the Martyr Saints of China)
